title: On A Country Laird.
content:
Bless Jesus Christ, O Cardoness,
            With grateful lifted eyes,
        Who said that not the soul alone
            But body too, must rise:
        For had he said, "the soul alone
            From death I will deliver;"
        Alas! alas! O Cardoness,
            Then thou hadst slept for ever.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Burns delivers a powerful performance in "On A Country Laird."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
